signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4371 (implied) — NZD leads G8 performance while CAD lags significantly, creating a 1.372% spread. The divergence is fundamentally anchored by the RBNZ hike bias vs BoC hold stance, providing a +0bp carry neutral base that favors NZD on policy momentum. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) vs RBNZ hike bias indicates a massive short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness. CAD weakness persists as the G8 laggard despite oil recovery attempts.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + NFP Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.68 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid despite Middle East tensions and US May operations to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade. While VIX at 17.1 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant, the pair faces immediate volatility from today's US Nonfarm Payrolls (exp. 62K). Intervention risk remains the primary ceiling if price tests 160.00.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7236 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction while retail sentiment is 86% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is aggressively fading the trend, increasing the risk of a retail capitulation spike if AUD extends gains. The 60bp AUD carry premium over USD (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) provides structural support. Watch for retail short covering to accelerate the move toward 0.7300.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Hawkishness
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1773 — Correlation with EuroStoxx has inverted to +0.722 (z=+1.78σ) against a negative historical norm, indicating EUR is trading as a 'risk-on' proxy rather than a funding currency. Hawkish ECB commentary regarding a data-dependent hiking path amid Middle East uncertainty supports the move. However, the 175bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage remains a structural headwind for sustained upside. Watch the 1.1800 level for rejection if NFP surprises to the upside.
